codeReview.summary: Runtime provider selection is now driven through a registry and fallback path, but the new end-to-end audit regression does not actually validate the renamed lifecycle event introduced by this fix.

issues.description: The feature changed the lifecycle audit action from `provider_called` to `provider_response_received`, but TestProviderRouteEndToEnd still only asserts the separate `provider_called` audit emitted by LogProviderCall. That leaves the specific regression this fix claims to cover—the renamed lifecycle audit carrying `provider_model`—untested, so a future change could silently break the new action without failing this suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note still says trusted review instructions are modeled as a single global `TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown` string, which is stale after the beta config correctness fix introduced per-path directory review handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/chris/workspace/mreviewer/.factory/library/architecture.md:28 — 'Trusted review instructions are still modeled as a single `TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown` string today' conflicts with the completed beta config fix and current rules behavior.

summary: Fail: runtime routing code looks sound, but the added audit regression coverage misses the actual renamed lifecycle event, leaving part of the claimed fix unverified. One non-blocking shared-state knowledge gap was also noted in .factory/library/architecture.md.
